2653.reason: Error: tokens.get() returns the next token after consumption, but should use the consumed token. The consumed token is returned by consumeExpect and should be stored in a local variable.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: tokens.get() returns the next token after consumption, but should use the consumed token. The consumed token is returned by consumeExpect and should be stored in a local variable.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: After consumeExpect, tokens.get() returns the next token, not the consumed one. Should use the returned Token from consumeExpect.

1722.reason: [Error] This causes lineNum to increment twice. The '\n' is left unconsumed, so the next getSym() call will skip it as whitespace and increment lineNum again. Remove this line or properly consume '\n' inside the loop.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error] This causes lineNum to increment twice. The '\n' is left unconsumed, so the next getSym() call will skip it as whitespace and increment lineNum again. Remove this line or properly consume '\n' inside the loop.

1791.reason: [Error] Missing retract(). If curChar is not '&', the next character has been consumed by getChar() but not put back into the stream. This causes the lexer to skip a character. Should call retract(); here.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error] Missing retract(). If curChar is not '&', the next character has been consumed by getChar() but not put back into the stream. This causes the lexer to skip a character. Should call retract(); here.

1800.reason: [Error] Missing retract(). Same issue as '&'. The non-'|' character is consumed and lost. Should call retract(); here.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error] Missing retract(). Same issue as '&'. The non-'|' character is consumed and lost. Should call retract(); here.

2661.reason: [Switch expression syntax (case X ->) requires Java 14+. If the compilation environment uses Java 8 or 11, this will cause a compilation error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Switch expression syntax (case X ->) requires Java 14+. If the compilation environment uses Java 8 or 11, this will cause a compilation error.]

2469.reason: [This condition is incorrect because it stops processing one token before the end of the token list. It should be 'index < tokenLists.size()' to include all tokens, otherwise the last token will never be processed which can cause parsing errors and missing semicolon detection issues.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect because it stops processing one token before the end of the token list. It should be 'index < tokenLists.size()' to include all tokens, otherwise the last token will never be processed which can cause parsing errors and missing semicolon detection issues.]

1874.reason: [The lookahead logic is incorrect: for a FuncDef like 'int foo()', the parser sees INTTK at current position and LPARENT at ahead(2), so it skips Decl parsing correctly, but for VarDecl like 'int a;', there is no LPARENT at ahead(2), so it parses as Decl. However, the condition fails when the declaration spans multiple lines or when token lookahead goes beyond available tokens, causing IndexOutOfBoundsException or misclassification. Also, MainFuncDef starts with 'int main', so when current token is INTTK and next is MAINTK, this condition would incorrectly treat it as a Decl if not handled before. The check for MainFuncDef should come before Decl/FuncDef checks.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The lookahead logic is incorrect: for a FuncDef like 'int foo()', the parser sees INTTK at current position and LPARENT at ahead(2), so it skips Decl parsing correctly, but for VarDecl like 'int a;', there is no LPARENT at ahead(2), so it parses as Decl. However, the condition fails when the declaration spans multiple lines or when token lookahead goes beyond available tokens, causing IndexOutOfBoundsException or misclassification. Also, MainFuncDef starts with 'int main', so when current token is INTTK and next is MAINTK, this condition would incorrectly treat it as a Decl if not handled before. The check for MainFuncDef should come before Decl/FuncDef checks.]
